differentials.name: Pylorus Inadequate gastric distention limits diagnostic evaluation and may create a false appearance of thickening or may obscure true disease.
differentials.raw: Pylorus Inadequate gastric distention limits diagnostic evaluation and may create a false appearance of thickening or may obscure true disease.

context: √  stomach bubble on CXR √  accumulation of intraluminal contents on CT √  ≤ 5 mm in thickness (in distended state) »  Incisura angularis =  transition to antral-type mucosa ◊  Most common site for gastric ulcers! √  ≤ 12 mm in thickness (in distended state) √  exhibits the most peristalsis √   may display mural stratification on CECT
